comparison.context: The EMA 9/21 baseline generated 11 trades in 7 days with a 63.6% win rate but still net negative PnL. The EMA 50/200 baseline generated only 1 trade in 90 days because the confluence requirements are very strict (EMA 50/200 trend alignment requires longer timeframes, and all 7 confluence factors must align in a single 15m candle to trigger a signal).

known_limitations: Only 1 trade in 90 days — insufficient sample size for statistical significance.
EMA 50/200 on 15m is extremely strict — requires alignment of EMA cross, pullback, ADX, structure, swing, and candle direction in a single bar.
90-day range includes a significant bearish trend (April 16 to July 14), which penalizes long-only entries.
No parameter optimization has been performed; weights and thresholds are defaults from the spec.
Slippage (2 bps) and fees (0.05%) are estimates.
